checks.finding: REQ-001 fixes the tool signature evidence_deep_verify(root,feature,taskId) with zod feature/taskId inputs; REQ-002 enumerates the exact six-value per-artifact status set (match/mismatch/missing/too-large/path-denied/invalid-recorded-sha) and the {path,recordedSha256,computedSha256?,status,reason?} shape; REQ-004/005/006/008 fix each response sub-object (artifactsDigest, specRevision, gitCommit, signature). Canonical formulas (evidence_canonical, compute_spec_revision, ^[0-9a-f]{40}$) are bound verbatim to the host scripts. Terms, actors, states, inputs and outputs are concrete enough that two implementers converge; no ambiguity gates design.

No requirement/AC/non-goal conflict. REQ-003's universal verdict rule (pass iff all artifacts match AND all in-process invariants hold) is consistent with AC-018's empty-artifacts vacuous pass (empty universal is true) and with AC-017 (invalid-recorded-sha contributes fail) and AC-019 (absent specs -> spec_revision '' match). The empty-set canonical digest in AC-018 is consistent with REQ-004's formula (SHA-256 of empty concatenation). Non-goals (no HMAC crypto verify per REQ-008/ADR-0008, no git ancestry per REQ-006/OQ-001) align with, not contradict, the verdict and echo-only rules.

Round-1 FAIL is genuinely closed with observable, non-vacuous, REQ/TEST-mapped ACs. AC-017 covers non-64-hex/empty recorded sha -> status invalid-recorded-sha with explicit no-misclassify-as-mismatch and no-throw guards and a combined-condition assertion (non-64-hex && missing still converges to fail), mapped REQ-002/003/011 + TEST-017. AC-018 covers empty artifacts[] via empty-set canonical digest and vacuous pass/fail without throw, mapped REQ-002/003/004/011 + TEST-018. AC-019 covers all-specs-absent spec_revision=='' with match/mismatch branches and no throw, mapped REQ-005/003/011 + TEST-019. All six REQ-002 enum statuses now have coverage (match AC-001; mismatch AC-002/003; missing AC-004; too-large/path-denied AC-005; invalid-recorded-sha AC-017). Other negative paths (spec drift AC-006, non-40-hex AC-007, external commit AC-008, crossbinding mismatch AC-009/010, no-key AC-011, error envelopes AC-015) are covered.

Assumptions are either resolved by reference or explicitly marked as decisions. Canonical-form/formula assumptions are resolved by verbatim quotation of generate/check-evidence-bundle.sh into design.md; the path-guard allowlist assumption is stated with the path-denied fallback; the 'without external tools' interpretation (git ancestry out of scope) is explicitly recorded as ADR-0008 and marked as a human-approval decision. OQ-001 carries an adopted Default with 'Blocks Implementation: no' and a stated resolution path, so no unresolved assumption blocks design or task decomposition.

No human-approval, governance, or irreversible-change boundary is in scope for this feature. The tool is strictly read-only (Non-goals bar writes/repair/signature-generation; REQ-011 routes all disk access through path-guard) and only surfaces an advisory verdict for a Done-transition go/no-go decision it neither performs nor gates. The Done transition itself is outside this feature's surface. SKIP condition (no such boundary in scope) is satisfied.

The specification hands off to design/implementation-policy review without requiring invention of product behavior: 13 REQs define precise response shapes, the verdict/failures rule (REQ-003), the NO-KEY/NO-SIGNATURE-VERIFY central control (REQ-008), the host-deferred git-ancestry echo (REQ-006), security boundaries B1-B3, a complete Edge Cases list, and 19 ACs each mapped to REQ and TEST IDs with observable outcomes. Formulas are deferred to design as verbatim quotes but the observable behavior is fully specified, so the design reviewer has enough to proceed.
